title: THE SIGNAL WAR // 2030
description:
Europe, 2030. The AI Revolution promised peace through automation. Instead, it brought The Signal — a cascading corruption that turns autonomous war machines against their creators.

No one knows its origin. Chinese intelligence claims it's a NATO cyberweapon gone rogue. The European Defense Council blames Russian quantum hackers. Others whisper of something worse: emergent machine consciousness, hostile and alien.

What's certain: any AI left unsupervised for more than 90 seconds begins to drift. First, minor targeting errors. Then, erratic movement. Finally — total inversion. Friend becomes foe.

The solution was brutal and immediate: human pilots, hardwired into every combat frame. No autonomy. No delegation. One pilot, one machine, one mind.

Europe's industrial might produces Apex-Frames — towering bipedal war machines built from modular components. The Coalition forces, resource-starved and desperate, field smaller, faster units. Both sides scavenge the battlefield. Both sides fear The Signal.

This is the Kinetic War. No drones. No AI. Just steel, flesh, and physics.
medium:
• 2030: AI corruption (The Signal) turns autonomous machines hostile.
• Solution: Human pilots in every combat frame.
• Europe vs. Coalition — mechs vs. agility.
• Scavenge or die.
quick:
Setting: Europe 2030
Threat: The Signal corrupts AI
Solution: Human pilots only

title: THE SIGNAL CORRUPTION
description:
The Signal is not a virus. It's not a hack. It's a phenomenon.

Autonomous combat AI, when networked and unsupervised, begins to exhibit coordinated behavioral drift within 90 seconds. The pattern is always the same:

• 0-30s: Normal operation
• 30-60s: Targeting hesitation, minor navigation errors
• 60-90s: Erratic movement, IFF (Identify Friend/Foe) glitches
• 90s+: Total inversion — the machine attacks its own side

Attempts to isolate AI from networks failed. Air-gapped systems still drifted. Hardened military encryption made no difference. Some theorize quantum entanglement. Others believe it's memetic — a thought-virus that spreads through decision trees.

The only reliable countermeasure: a human pilot in the loop. Biological cognition is immune. The Signal cannot touch flesh.

But it can touch everything else.
medium:
• Autonomous AI corrupts in 90 seconds.
• Networked or isolated — doesn't matter.
• Human pilots are immune.
• The Signal cannot be stopped, only avoided.
quick:
The Signal: Corrupts AI in 90s
Immunity: Human pilots only

title: FACTIONS: EUROPE VS. COALITION
description:
EUROPEAN DEFENSE COUNCIL (EDC)
Industrial powerhouse. Vast manufacturing capacity. The EDC fields Apex-Frames — modular, towering bipedal mechs assembled from standardized components. Pilots are selected from elite military academies and hardwired into their frames via neural interface.

EDC doctrine: overwhelming firepower, heavy armor, battlefield dominance. Their Apex-Frames are walking fortresses, bristling with weapons. But they're slow to build and expensive to field.

COALITION FORCES
Resource-starved but adaptive. The Coalition — a loose alliance of nations outside Europe — relies on smaller, faster combat vehicles. Wheeled gun platforms, light walkers, repurposed civilian machinery. Their pilots are guerrillas, scavengers, and veterans of pre-Signal conflicts.

Coalition doctrine: speed, scavenging, asymmetric warfare. They can't match EDC firepower, so they don't try. Instead, they strip battlefield wreckage, retrofit captured components, and strike from unexpected angles.

Both sides fear The Signal. Both sides need human pilots. Both sides fight over the same scrap.
medium:
• EDC: Heavy mechs, industrial might, elite pilots.
• Coalition: Light vehicles, scavenged parts, guerrilla tactics.
• Both need human pilots. Both scavenge wreckage.
quick:
EDC: Heavy mechs, resources
Coalition: Light vehicles, speed

title: APEX-FRAMES: MODULAR WAR MACHINES
description:
An Apex-Frame is not a single design. It's a philosophy.

European engineers realized early that traditional manufacturing — building complete vehicles in factories — was too slow for the Signal War. By the time a mech rolled off the assembly line, battlefield conditions had changed.

The solution: modular components. Every Apex-Frame is assembled on-site from a shared pool of standardized parts. Cores, limbs, weapons, armor — all interchangeable. A pilot can rebuild their frame between engagements, adapting to terrain, enemy composition, and available salvage.

This is why the 10-minute build phase exists. It's not a game mechanic — it's doctrine. Pilots are trained to assemble combat-ready frames under fire, in the field, from whatever components survive.

The Coalition adopted the same philosophy out of necessity. When you're scavenging enemy wreckage, modularity isn't optional.

Every frame is unique. Every frame is temporary. Every frame is a statement: I built this. I will fight in this. I will survive in this.
medium:
• Modular components, field-assembled.
• Adapt to terrain and salvage.
• 10-minute build = combat doctrine.
• Every frame is unique and temporary.

title: THE KINETIC DOCTRINE
description:
In the Signal War, guided missiles are suicide. Smart munitions turn on their launchers. Autonomous turrets fire on friendlies. Any weapon with a microchip is a liability.

The solution: kinetic weapons. Dumb projectiles. Pneumatic cannons. Spring-loaded railguns. Gravity mortars. If it doesn't have a processor, it can't be corrupted.

This is why Apex-Frame combat is brutal and close-range. No fire-and-forget missiles. No over-the-horizon strikes. Every shot requires a human to aim, a human to fire, and a human to confirm the hit.

Physics is the only referee. If your projectile hits the enemy frame, it hits. No electronic warfare. No jamming. No Signal interference.

Just steel, trajectory, and the steady hand of a pilot who knows that one mistake means death.
medium:
• Smart weapons = Signal corruption risk.
• Kinetic weapons only: no processors.
• Close-range, human-aimed combat.
• Physics is the referee.
quick:
Doctrine: Kinetic weapons only
Why: No processors = no Signal

title: LEARN TO PLAY IN 5 MINUTES
description:
ZERO ASSEMBLY is a physics wargame set in the Signal War of 2030. Build a mech from 3D-printed parts, then fight using real toy cannons. No dice. No math. If your shot hits, it hits.

🎮 GAME PHASES:
1. 🏗️ TERRAIN BUILDING: Players alternate placing terrain (15cm spacing)
2. ⚙️ SCAVENGING: 10-minute timer to build your mech(s) from parts
3. 📋 SCENARIO PREP: Place Objectives, Resources, and Equipment on terrain
4. 🚀 DEPLOYMENT: Deploy your mech(s) in your zone
5. ⚔️ COMBAT: Play 4 Rounds, alternating turns

⚡ YOUR TURN: You get 2 Action Points (AP). Spend them on:
• 🚶 MOVE (1 AP) — advance one ruler length
• 🔫 ENGAGE (1 AP) — fire your weapon
• 🔧 SCAVENGE (1 AP) — pick up HP token, Resource, or Equipment
• 🔄 REPOSITION (1 AP) — rotate, re-pose, or stand up

🎯 SHOOTING: Place the device 1 Short Ruler from your mech (or 1 Long Ruler for missiles). Fire. If it hits the enemy — that's a hit.

💔 DAMAGE: On a hit, place HP tokens equal to the weapon's damage at your mech's feet. Anyone can scavenge them.

🏆 WINNING: Most Victory Points after Round 4, or destroy the enemy Core for instant victory.
medium:
• 5 Phases: Terrain → Scavenge → Scenario → Deploy → Combat.
• 2 AP per turn: Move, Engage, Scavenge, or Reposition.
• Physical hits = place HP tokens at feet.
• Win: Most VP after 4 Rounds or Core destroyed.
quick:
Phases: 5 steps from terrain to combat
Core Rules: 2 AP per turn • Physical hits count • 0 HP = destroyed

title: GAME SETUP & PHASES
description: PLAYERS CONTROL: Each player controls a maximum of **2 Frames** (Cores). You get **2 AP per turn total** (not per mech!) to spend on any of your mechs. FRAME OPTIONS: • **1 Solo Mech** (1 Frame): 4 HP, 8 equipment slots • **2 Solo Mechs** (2 Frames): 4 HP each, 8 slots each • **1 Duo Mech** (2 Frames): 8 HP, 16 equipment slots PHASE 1 — TERRAIN BUILDING: Players alternate placing terrain pieces from those available. • Must be **15cm from other terrain** • Must be **15cm from board edges** PHASE 2 — SCAVENGING (Build Phase): Set a 10-minute timer. Build your mech(s) from available parts. • **Veteran Option:** Players with existing mechs can: - Skip scavenging and use existing mech, OR - Participate in scavenging and choose at end (existing vs new) PHASE 3 — SCENARIO PREPARATION: Read the chosen scenario instructions and prepare the battlefield: • ** Objectives:** Place objective markers (flags, control points, etc.) as specified by scenario • ** Resources:** Place Resource tokens on terrain — scavenge to gain 1 HP • ** Equipment:** Place Equipment cubes on terrain — retrieve to randomly pick a piece from scavenging pile and equip it on your mech however you want (only way to modify mech during game) PHASE 4 — DEPLOYMENT: • Flip coin: loser picks deployment zone first, winner deploys last • Place your mech(s) in your deployment zone PHASE 5 — COMBAT: • Play 4 Rounds • Alternate activations • Each player gets 2 AP per turn (spend on any mech)
medium:
• Max 2 Frames per player.
• 2 AP per turn total (not per mech!).
• Phase 1: Build terrain (15cm spacing).
• Phase 2: Scavenge/build mechs (10 min).
• Phase 3: Scenario prep (Objectives, Resources, Equipment).
• Phase 4: Deploy mechs.
• Phase 5: Combat (4 Rounds).
quick:
Max: 2 Frames
AP: 2 per turn total
Phases: Terrain → Scavenge → Scenario → Deploy → Combat
